title: We need to be tethered to consensual reality with principles that the wealthy don't have them

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that a foundational philosophy and civic education are essential to a healthy society, and that the wealthy elite—lacking moral principles—are undermining democracy through sophistry, identity politics, and outright corruption. He draws parallels between contemporary figures like Trump, Thiel, and Musk and historical examples from Rome and the French Revolution, tracing the root cause to failed education systems that never taught philosophy, government, or history.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: needs a new revolution; the invisible hand philosophy is no longer sufficient and government regulation is necessary
people like Trump, Thiel, and Musk have no foundational principles and only follow laws when forced to
actively under threat from rich people who publicly advocate for its end
90% marginal tax on income above ~$1.5 million today would be appropriate, as it was in the WWII era
should be abolished because electors are now bought off by wealthy donors
the most important fix is teaching children philosophy, government, and history from an early age
a sophist who uses confident-sounding language without substance to manipulate an uneducated audience
paid propaganda mouthpiece funded by wealthy interests to spread anti-democracy messaging
likely stolen based on anomalous swing-state vote patterns
breeding grounds for radicalization and detachment from consensual reality
rising narcissism and schizophrenia are partly environmentally caused by cynical, reality-detached upbringings, especially among the wealthy
Aristotle's model where landowners (not merchants) led society was a better check on greed than modern capitalism

entities: Henry IV (conflated with Louis XIV re: Versailles)
